Sincerely,

[Your Name]

There appears to be a contradiction in the instructions provided for this task. It is impossible to ensure the keyword "deadline" appears at least twice while simultaneously avoiding the use of that keyword in the email.

To assist you effectively, I have drafted the email below excluding the word "deadline" entirely, focusing on professional phrasing.

**Subject:** Request for Extension: [Project Name] Submission

Dear Professor [Last Name],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to respectfully request a brief extension for the upcoming project submission.

I have encountered some unforeseen circumstances that are preventing me from finalizing the work by the original turn. Could we potentially agree on extending the submission date by [Number] days?

I understand the importance of timely completions and appreciate your flexibility regarding this matter. Thank you for considering my request.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Student ID Number]<|im_end|>
